meaning: except (what follows is excluded from the previous ruling)
they regularly protect/guard (referring to those who maintain their prayers)
the inheritors (group of men who inherit)
they (referring to those who inherit Paradise)
those who last forever (plural, in regular case)
from a pure extract (referring to how humans were created)
the small piece of chewed flesh
so we covered (the bones with flesh)
then (after a time gap, connecting this creation stage to the previous one)
we created (the seven paths above you)
paths, ways (being described in this ayah)
and so we caused it to settle/dwell (referring to the water mentioned in previous ayah)
and indeed we (able to make the water go away)
for you all (referring to the gardens being created for the addressees)
and a dye/oil (for those who eat)
we give drink to you all (referring to the livestock mentioned in the ayah)
and upon her/it (referring to the animals mentioned earlier)
you (all) are carried (on ships and animals)
like you all (referring to the disbelievers being addressed)
surely He would have sent down
angels (as the object being acted upon)
by our eyes (watching over the construction of the ark)

from them (referring to the wrongdoers mentioned in the next ayah)
those who (referring to the wrongdoers in this ayah)
the ship (that is being boarded in this ayah)
who/which/that (referring to the one who saved us)
the people (who are being saved from)
then (connects this ayah to what came before it)
others (referring to another generation after the previous one)
in them (referring to the people mentioned before)
and we made them live luxuriously
from him/it (referring to what the disbelievers eat)
if (introducing the first part of the statement 'it is nothing but our worldly life')
and not (we are not believers in him)
with/by the truth (in this ayah, describes how the punishment took them)
they denied him forcefully (referring to their messenger)
then (connects this event to what happened before)
and their people (referring to two people)
and so they strongly denied/rejected them both
and they were (referring to those who rejected the messengers)
the destroyed ones (those who were destroyed)
so that they might, perhaps they will
and we made (something into something else)
the pure and good things (referring to lawful foods)
and indeed (connects to previous statement while emphasizing what follows)
your community/nation (referring to all the prophets' communities mentioned in this ayah)
your Lord (referring to all those being addressed)
so leave them! (referring to the disbelievers)
we extend/provide to them (referring to those who think their wealth means Allah approves of them)
those who are returning (to their Lord)
the good things (that they race towards)
in a state of being overwhelmed
that, that one (pointing to something far)
they cry out in distress (when the punishment comes)
spending time talking at night (describing their state)
the truth (as the subject of the sentence)
rather/but rather (contradicting the previous statement about following desires)
by their remembrance (of the message they were given)
you ask them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
and indeed (connects to previous statement while emphasizing what follows)
those who (plural, referring to those who don't believe in the Hereafter)
in/by the Hereafter (those who don't believe in)
with them (referring to those who reject faith)
they wander blindly (referring to the disbelievers in the ayah)
and not (they did not humble themselves to their Lord)
this (pointing to something specific in the ayah)
when (introduces the opening of a gate of punishment)
they/them (referring to the disbelievers in this ayah)
those who have lost all hope
little (as a description of how much you do something)
you all thank (referring to the people being addressed in this verse about their hearing, sight, and hearts)
the first ones (who said similar things before)
and we were (past state before being bones and dust)
surely we will be raised up
this (referring to what was mentioned before about previous warnings)
then will you not? (questioning why they don't remember)
you all were (addressing multiple people in the past)
then how (are you being deluded away from the truth)
any god (in context: if there were other gods)
surely he would have gone away (hypothetical past)
he created (what each god would have created)
they describe (referring to those who attribute false things to Allah)
and indeed we (are able to show you what We promise them)
that (introduces what Allah is capable of doing)
they describe (referring to those who attribute false qualities to Allah)
one of them (when death comes to)
in what (referring to what was left behind)
a barrier (between this world and the next)

so those (referring to those whose scales are light)
Hell (as the object being referred to)
you (all) speak to me (don't speak to me!)
